_note: Hungarian orthography → IPA mapping. Apply longest-match-first: check trigraphs before digraphs before single characters. 'ly' is mapped to /j/ (modern standard Hungarian pronunciation).
_gy_ty_note: Hungarian 'gy' /ɟ/ (voiced palatal stop) and 'ty' /c/ (voiceless palatal stop) are not in the standard Erben phoneme set. For feature classification purposes: gy → voiced + palatal + stop; ty → voiceless + palatal + stop. These should be added to phoneme_features.json if not already present.
_gemination_note: Doubled consonants in Hungarian spelling (e.g., 'tt', 'nn', 'ssz', 'ccs') represent gemination (long consonants). For Erben purposes, treat as single phoneme — the macro-concept activation is the same regardless of length.
